beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. An AI-generated dialogue lets Emma reflect on her own character development. Austen appears alongside her to explain why she wrote Emma that way. The exchange teaches how to navigate workplace exploitation and resistance. The piece is explicitly labeled as AI-generated, set against an era barring women from university.

artifactLines: An AI-generated dialogue lets Emma reflect on her own character development.
Austen appears alongside her to explain why she wrote Emma that way.
The exchange teaches how to navigate workplace exploitation and resistance.
The piece is explicitly labeled as AI-generated, set against an era barring women from university.

beats.narration_text: Having Emma reflect on her own development while Austen explains why she wrote her that way, all openly labeled AI-generated, is a striking teaching device, especially tied to a time when women could not attend university. I want to build a small version. Help me: (1) outline a dialogue where character and author disagree about the same scene; (2) decide what the AI label should disclose to keep it honest; (3) pick which resistance-to-exploitation lesson the split viewpoint teaches best.
